jokes.text: You hear about that chemical factory explosion outside of Colorado Springs? No. Good.
jokes.setup: Jack casually asks about a chemical factory explosion
jokes.punchline: His relief that Liz hasn't heard about it implies NBC corporate is somehow involved
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate cynicism delivered with matter-of-fact darkness. Economy is excellent - three words deliver maximum dread

jokes.text: it's best to trlightly
jokes.setup: Liz tries to say 'tread lightly'
jokes.punchline: She stumbles over the phrase, creating 'trlightly'
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's nervousness through verbal stumbling. Simple but character-appropriate

jokes.text: That is what we in the show business like to call a 'cameo.'
jokes.setup: Jack explains Tracy's chaotic appearance
jokes.punchline: Calling it a professional 'cameo' when it was clearly unplanned chaos
jokes.explanation: Jack's corporate spin on obvious disaster. The formal 'we in show business' makes it funnier

jokes.text: 'Cause you look good, like a solid gold candy bar!
jokes.setup: Tracy making small talk with NBC tour group
jokes.punchline: Completely nonsensical comparison to a solid gold candy bar
jokes.explanation: Peak Tracy absurdism. The specificity of 'solid gold candy bar' is perfect random word association

jokes.text: within his remunerative body of work
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to sound intellectual about Tracy's career
jokes.punchline: Uses 'remunerative' (relating to payment) instead of a word that makes sense
jokes.explanation: Jenna's pretentiousness showing through malapropism. Character-specific humor

jokes.text: Give up the butt, ladies. Give up the butt.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions Tracy's stand-up
jokes.punchline: This is apparently an example of his 'comedic' material
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's crude comedy style. The cringe is the point but it's not particularly clever

jokes.text: He's an imbecile. Well, he's our imbecile now.
jokes.setup: Jenna calls Tracy an imbecile
jokes.punchline: Liz's resigned acceptance with 'our imbecile'
jokes.explanation: Great resignation humor. 'Our imbecile' captures the workplace comedy dynamic perfectly

jokes.text: I'm just surprised he's still got his shirt-- Oh, there it goes.
jokes.setup: Liz commenting on Tracy keeping his shirt on
jokes.punchline: Tracy takes his shirt off at that exact moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing. The immediate payoff of the setup is expertly executed

jokes.text: Don't look at me! Do not look at me in the eyes. Do not look at Mr. Jordan!
jokes.setup: Tracy arrives with an entourage
jokes.punchline: They're treating him like a deity who can't be looked upon
jokes.explanation: Absurd celebrity worship taken to religious extremes. Good escalation from don't look to don't look in eyes

jokes.text: You smoke weed, right, Pete? Uh... No. Yeah, me neither. Me and you, Pete. Me and you.
jokes.setup: Tracy asks Pete about smoking weed
jokes.punchline: Bonds with him over not smoking weed, which is obviously a lie
jokes.explanation: Tracy's transparent lying combined with instant fake bonding. Good character establishment

jokes.text: Relax your balls, Pete.
jokes.setup: Pete is nervous about being fired
jokes.punchline: Tracy's advice is to 'relax your balls'
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Tracy wisdom. Three words of complete nonsense that somehow sound like advice

jokes.text: Well if you ever want to piss off your parents, you come see me.
jokes.setup: Tracy meeting the writing staff
jokes.punchline: Offers himself as a way to disappoint parents
jokes.explanation: Dark self-awareness from Tracy about his reputation. Good character insight

jokes.text: Frank Rossitano has every copy of Black Tail magazine ever published.
jokes.setup: Liz introducing staff to Tracy with personal details
jokes.punchline: Shares Frank's embarrassing porn collection as an introduction
jokes.explanation: Awkward oversharing disguised as professional introduction. Shows Liz's management inexperience

jokes.text: James Spurlock, but we call him Toofer, because with him you get a 'Two for One,' he's a black guy and a Harvard guy.
jokes.setup: Liz introducing Toofer
jokes.punchline: Explains his nickname reduces him to racial/educational stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of workplace tokenism. The casual way Liz explains it makes the cringe perfect

jokes.text: Jerry from set design. Wally, cue cards. Rupert Murdoch. Melinda Gates. Ziggy from the cartoon Ziggy.
jokes.setup: Jack demonstrating his memory by naming staff
jokes.punchline: Seamlessly includes celebrities and cartoon characters as if they're NBC employees
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that starts realistic then becomes completely absurd. 'Ziggy from the cartoon Ziggy' is peak specificity

jokes.text: I got a character named Biscuit. Write that up. Got another character named Raylondo, who's a two-foot tall Spanish hustler.
jokes.setup: Tracy pitching character ideas
jokes.punchline: Increasingly ridiculous and potentially offensive character concepts
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's questionable creative instincts. The specificity of 'two-foot tall Spanish hustler' is good

jokes.text: Got another character named Ching Chong, who loves to play Ping Pong. I just made that up right now. 'Cause that's how I flow.
jokes.setup: Tracy continuing his character pitches
jokes.punchline: Proudly presents an obviously offensive stereotype he invented on the spot
jokes.explanation: The cringe comes from Tracy's pride in his terrible impromptu racism. Shows his complete lack of awareness

jokes.text: Do you know that he once got arrested for walking naked through LaGuardia? And that he once fell asleep on Ted Danson's roof?
jokes.setup: Jenna listing Tracy's crazy behavior
jokes.punchline: Specific absurd incidents that get progressively weirder
jokes.explanation: Great specificity - naked at LaGuardia and Ted Danson's roof are perfectly random celebrity stories

jokes.text: He bit Dakota Fanning on the face! When you hear his version, she was kind of asking for it.
jokes.setup: More Tracy stories culminating in biting a child actress
jokes.punchline: Liz's defense implies the child actress provoked it
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to absurdity. The idea that Dakota Fanning 'was asking for it' is brilliantly wrong

jokes.text: Stop falling in love with gay guys?
jokes.setup: Jack asks what else he told Jenna
jokes.punchline: Jenna's personal romantic pattern becomes advice
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail delivered casually. Shows Jenna's romantic history and obliviousness

jokes.text: Glaub mir. Diese 'Advantium Microwaves' sind die beste.
jokes.setup: Jack is on a business call
jokes.punchline: Speaking German about microwaves while dealing with Tracy crisis
jokes.explanation: Shows Jack's corporate multinational dealings. The German adds sophistication that contrasts with Tracy chaos

jokes.text: The Girlie Show is now T.G.S. with Tracy Jordan?
jokes.setup: Liz discovers the show name change
jokes.punchline: Complete rebranding around Tracy without consulting her
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of corporate takeover. Shows how quickly Tracy has taken over everything

jokes.text: If you say you like it, you can have some pizza. You people like pizza?
jokes.setup: Focus group testing the name change
jokes.punchline: Bribing participants with pizza and casual racism
jokes.explanation: Good satire of terrible focus group methodology and casual prejudice

jokes.text: Then we could sit around and braid each other's hair until we get our periods at the same time.
jokes.setup: Liz wants Jack to help smooth things with Jenna
jokes.punchline: Jack's dismissive comparison to stereotypical female bonding
jokes.explanation: Jack's masculine dismissal of emotional management. Character-appropriate condescension

jokes.text: Then I'll do what my father did when I was two. Lure you to the edge of the pool with a puppy, and push you in.
jokes.setup: Liz asks what if she can't swim
jokes.punchline: Jack's traumatic childhood swimming lesson involving puppy deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack backstory - combines corporate metaphor with genuinely disturbing childhood trauma. The puppy detail makes it memorable

jokes.text: Freaky deakies need love too. Freaky deakies need love too.
jokes.setup: Tracy telling a story about Rick
jokes.punchline: The memorable phrase about unconventional people deserving love
jokes.explanation: Highly quotable phrase that became a catchphrase. Simple but memorable wisdom from Tracy

jokes.text: Unh-unh. Superman does good. You're doing well. You need to study your grammar, son.
jokes.setup: Tracy says he's doing good
jokes.punchline: Toofer corrects his grammar with Superman reference
jokes.explanation: Perfect Toofer character moment - intellectually superior but petty. The Superman reference makes the grammar lesson memorable

jokes.text: Wow. That was embarrassing for you.
jokes.setup: Toofer corrects Tracy's grammar
jokes.punchline: Tracy completely misses that he was corrected and thinks Toofer embarrassed himself
jokes.explanation: Great character work - Tracy's complete obliviousness to being corrected. Maximum impact with minimal words

jokes.text: And I'm proud as a peacork, baby.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to say his line correctly
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces peacock as 'peacork'
jokes.explanation: Simple mispronunciation humor. Works because it shows Tracy's inability to handle basic tasks

jokes.text: Right, Jenna Mulvaney? Maroney. Rhymes with Baloney.
jokes.setup: Tracy trying to remember Jenna's name
jokes.punchline: Gets it wrong and Liz's helpful rhyme makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Sets up the running gag of Tracy not knowing Jenna's name. The baloney rhyme backfires

jokes.text: And I'm proud as a peacock. Right, Mabaloney?
jokes.setup: Tracy finally says peacock correctly
jokes.punchline: But now calls Jenna 'Mabaloney' based on the rhyme
jokes.explanation: Great callback escalation. 'Mabaloney' is perfect - combines the rhyme with Tracy's inability to remember names

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Tracy Jordan. I'm black, NBC. Very proud, like peacocks. Right, Janet?
jokes.setup: Final take of the promo
jokes.punchline: Completely garbles the line and calls Jenna 'Janet'
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation to complete incompetence. 'I'm black, NBC' is wonderfully garbled

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Tracy Jordan. I'm black, NBC. Very proud, like peacocks. Right, Janet? I think we got it.
jokes.setup: Final attempt at the promo
jokes.punchline: Complete breakdown into fragmented nonsense with wrong name again
jokes.explanation: Perfect culmination of the escalating failure. The grammatical breakdown is brilliantly absurd.

jokes.text: Frank also loves that video of the monkey smelling his own butt.
jokes.setup: Defending Frank liking Tracy's movies
jokes.punchline: Points out Frank's terrible taste in entertainment
jokes.explanation: Simple lowbrow humor establishing Frank's character. Functional but not sophisticated

jokes.text: Oh my God. I didn't use the N word, did I? Oh, no, no, no, Miss Maroney, you did not.
jokes.setup: Jenna realizes her mic was on
jokes.punchline: Her first concern is whether she said something racist
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jenna's narcissistic panic. The sound guy's polite response adds to the cringe

jokes.text: This is my office. Really? I see you bring a little feminine magic to everything you touch.
jokes.setup: Jack finds Liz hiding in storage closet
jokes.punchline: Her 'office' is clearly just a supply closet
jokes.explanation: Good visual humor about Liz's low status. Jack's condescending 'feminine magic' comment perfect

jokes.text: No, he's not your employee, he's your product.
jokes.setup: Liz calls Tracy her employee
jokes.punchline: Jack corrects her with corporate dehumanization
jokes.explanation: Perfect corporate cynicism. Reduces human relationships to business terms with chilling efficiency

jokes.text: I don't know. I'm a non-genius.
jokes.setup: Liz asks how to fix things
jokes.punchline: Jack sarcastically refers to Jenna calling him a non-genius
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Jenna's earlier insult. Shows Jack heard everything and is mildly offended

jokes.text: A crowd once threw a motorcycle at me.
jokes.setup: Tracy saying he can't be hurt by words
jokes.punchline: His example is physical violence with an implausible weapon
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy absurdity. The specificity of a motorcycle makes it memorable and implausible

jokes.text: And Toofer's just afraid of black people. Which one is Toofer? The black guy. Oh.
jokes.setup: Liz explaining Toofer's attitude toward Tracy
jokes.punchline: The irony that the black writer is afraid of black people
jokes.explanation: Smart racial humor that plays with stereotypes. Tracy's simple 'Oh' is perfect

jokes.text: Son of a mother!
jokes.setup: Liz realizing she's been broadcast again
jokes.punchline: Her attempt to avoid swearing results in nonsensical phrase
jokes.explanation: Good Liz character moment. Her clean alternative to cursing is endearingly awkward

jokes.text: Paranoid? Well, that just confirms all my suspicions.
jokes.setup: Jenna hearing she was called paranoid
jokes.punchline: Her response proves the point while missing the irony
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment where Jenna proves her paranoia by being paranoid about being called paranoid.

jokes.text: Nothing that plugs in, you guys! Nothing that could really hurt me!
jokes.setup: Writers throwing things at Liz
jokes.punchline: Her increasingly specific safety requirements while being pelted
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of physical comedy. The specific safety guidelines during assault are funny

jokes.text: Let's crash my car to see if the air-bags go off.
jokes.setup: Tracy suggesting they do something together
jokes.punchline: His idea is potentially lethal car destruction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic - destructive behavior as entertainment. Casual delivery makes it funnier

jokes.text: I got a yacht. I got a solid gold jet-ski. Two Batmobiles. The AIDS monkey's bones...
jokes.setup: Tracy listing his possessions
jokes.punchline: Goes from yacht to gold jet-ski to Batmobiles to monkey bones
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from believable to absurd to disturbing. 'AIDS monkey's bones' is peak random wealth

jokes.text: Miami, Tokyo, Denver.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing where he's taken his yacht
jokes.punchline: Includes landlocked Denver with coastal cities
jokes.explanation: Perfect subtle absurdity. Denver among yacht destinations is beautifully wrong with no explanation

jokes.text: Oh, that's Spanish for 'remember your mother.' No, I don't think it is.
jokes.setup: Jenna asks about the boat's Italian name
jokes.punchline: Tracy's confident but wrong translation
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy ignorance moment. Confidently wrong about basic facts. Jenna's polite correction is perfect

jokes.text: Did you know that all the writers in that magazine are also conductors?
jokes.setup: Tracy discussing Amtrak Magazine
jokes.punchline: His random 'fact' about train magazine writers
jokes.explanation: Classic Tracy made-up fact delivered with confidence. The specificity makes it memorable

jokes.text: Grizz is driving the boat? Don't worry. He was in the Navy.
jokes.setup: Concern about boat operation
jokes.punchline: Reassurance that turns out to be meaningless later
jokes.explanation: Sets up false security that becomes ironic when boat is stolen.

jokes.text: What's the deal with pudding? I'm Bill Cosby. Jell-o! Sweaters!
jokes.setup: Tracy doing celebrity impressions
jokes.punchline: Terrible Seinfeld/Cosby impressions reduced to catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Shows Tracy's poor impression skills. Just shouting random associated words instead of actual impressions

jokes.text: Like Batman and Robin. Like chicken and a chicken container.
jokes.setup: Tracy describing their team dynamic
jokes.punchline: Goes from classic duo to chicken and its container
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy logic escalation. 'Chicken and a chicken container' is beautifully specific and wrong

jokes.text: Like chicken and a chicken container.
jokes.setup: Tracy comparing their partnership to famous duos
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical comparison to food and its packaging
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Tracy absurdism. The specificity of 'chicken container' instead of 'bucket' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Por favor, no me dueles. Me llamo María.
jokes.setup: Hidden Spanish woman on the yacht
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tracy stole someone's boat with people still on it
jokes.explanation: Great plot twist reveal. Explains all the earlier yacht inconsistencies with perfect escalation

jokes.text: It's Spanish for, 'Remember Your Mother.' I've taken this boat to Denver. You can have that scotch. Grizz was in the Navy!
jokes.setup: Liz realizing all Tracy's claims about the yacht
jokes.punchline: Rapid-fire callback to every lie he told
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff connecting multiple earlier jokes. Shows how all Tracy's stories were covering his theft

jokes.text: Look how thin I look. And look how many emails I've gotten. It's not even 8:00 AM in L.A.
jokes.setup: Jenna sees her embarrassing newspaper photo
jokes.punchline: She's thrilled by the attention despite the negative context
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna vanity. Only cares about looking thin and getting attention, not the scandal context
